items.title: Indiana University and Zimmer Biomet launch research, workforce partnership
items.summary: Indiana University and Zimmer Biomet launched a three-year collaboration supporting research, workforce development, and innovation in joint replacement, bone healing, and mobility.
items.imageAlt: Indiana University and Zimmer Biomet representatives at a partnership signing event.

items.title: Kelley Indy students shape healthcare innovation with Zimmer Biomet
items.summary: Kelley Indianapolis Evening MBA students worked with Zimmer Biomet on AI-first ideas for orthopedic innovation, supply-chain performance, and connected medical devices.
items.imageAlt: Indiana University and Zimmer Biomet representatives walk through a Zimmer Biomet manufacturing facility in Warsaw, Indiana.

items.title: IU's musculoskeletal research brings better health solutions to communities and collaborators
items.summary: Researchers across disciplines at Indiana University are driving bioscience innovations that restore and strengthen mobility, from bone recovery technologies to the FIT Core and MSKMobile.
items.imageAlt: IU researchers conduct musculoskeletal function and imaging assessments in the FIT Core.

items.title: IMPACT advances bone and muscle health through research, technology and training
items.summary: An interdisciplinary partnership between Indiana University researchers and orthopedic industry leaders is advancing IMPACT’s mission to restore health for patients with osteoporosis, arthritis, and other musculoskeletal disorders.
items.imageAlt: IMPACT and IU researchers visit the Zimmer Biomet facility in Warsaw, Indiana.

items.title: Kelley professor part of NSF finalist consortium driving musculoskeletal innovation
items.summary: Kelley Indianapolis professor Amrou Awaysheh is part of the IU-led IMPACT consortium, which aims to build a musculoskeletal health innovation triangle across Indiana.
items.imageAlt: Amrou Awaysheh, Kelley School of Business professor and IMPACT technology and innovation leader.

items.title: IU-led consortium named NSF finalist for leadership in musculoskeletal healthcare innovation
items.summary: Indiana University announced that the IU-led IMPACT consortium was named one of 15 finalists in the NSF Regional Innovation Engines competition.
items.imageAlt: Melissa Kacena at an IMPACT booth representing the NSF finalist consortium.

items.title: IU orthopedic experts join BioCrossroads, OrthoWorx and Zimmer Biomet at FrameWORX Summit
items.summary: IU orthopedic experts joined BioCrossroads, OrthoWorx, and Zimmer Biomet for a FrameWORX Summit focused on musculoskeletal health innovation and Indiana’s orthopedic leadership.
items.imageAlt: Melissa Kacena and Roman Natoli, IU orthopedic experts featured for the BioCrossroads FrameWORX Summit.

items.title: IU scientists develop new bone marrow imaging technique
items.summary: IU scientists developed a new imaging technique that can visualize many cellular markers inside intact bone marrow tissue, supporting research into cancer, autoimmune disease, and musculoskeletal disorders.
items.imageAlt: IU scientist working with bone marrow imaging equipment.

items.title: IU School of Medicine names new director of Indiana Center for Musculoskeletal Health
items.summary: Melissa A. Kacena, PhD, was named director of the Indiana Center for Musculoskeletal Health, which focuses on new treatments, diagnostics, and technologies for bone, muscle, and cartilage disorders.
items.imageAlt: Melissa Kacena, director of the Indiana Center for Musculoskeletal Health.

items.title: NIH Small Business 101: Building Your Budget
items.summary: NIH SEED’s Small Business 101 webinar series includes a session on building budgets for SBIR/STTR applications and preparing for the next chapter of the small business program.
items.imageAlt: NIH SEED webinar graphic representing small business funding and commercialization support.

items.title: NIH Small Business 101: Managing Foreign Risk
items.summary: NIH SEED’s Small Business 101 webinar series includes a session on managing foreign risk for small businesses preparing SBIR/STTR applications.
items.imageAlt: NIH SEED webinar graphic representing small business funding and commercialization support.

items.title: Medical Devices in Indiana: Igniting the Next Wave of Growth
items.summary: BioCrossroads FrameWORX will bring medical device manufacturers, life sciences leaders, startups, academic partners, workforce experts, and economic-development leaders to Bloomington.
items.imageAlt: BioCrossroads FrameWORX August 2026 event graphic.

items.title: Write a Stronger NIH SBIR Specific Aims Page
items.summary: This hands-on BioCrossroads workshop is designed to help life sciences innovators draft a clearer NIH SBIR Specific Aims page and strengthen the commercial relevance of their proposal.
items.imageAlt: BioCrossroads and Indiana Biosciences Research Institute event graphic.

items.title: Indiana AI Innovation Network: Navigating AI Supply Chain Risks
items.summary: TechPoint’s Indiana AI Innovation Network will host a case-study review on identifying, managing, and mitigating AI supply chain risks using AI Bills of Materials.
items.imageAlt: TechPoint logo representing the Indiana AI Innovation Network event.

items.title: AJA 2026 / 2nd Annual ICMH Conference
items.summary: The 6th Annual Jaseng Academic International Conference is co-hosted by Jaseng and Indiana University and also serves as the 2nd Annual Indiana Center for Musculoskeletal Health Conference.
items.imageAlt: AJA 2026 conference graphic for the ICMH conference in Indianapolis.

items.title: Warsaw Medtech Expo - Batch 5
items.summary: Plug and Play Warsaw’s Medtech Expo will showcase Batch 5 startups and convene participants around healthcare and medical technology innovation in Winona Lake.
items.imageAlt: Plug and Play graphic representing the Warsaw Medtech Expo.

items.title: Midwest AI Summit
items.summary: Hosted by Prediction Guard, the Midwest AI Summit is focused on practical enterprise AI use cases in industries including healthcare, life sciences, manufacturing, and agriculture.
items.imageAlt: TechPoint logo representing the Midwest AI Summit.

items.title: Warsaw Plug-In Coffee Hour
items.summary: Plug and Play Warsaw will host a coffee-hour networking event at Zimmer Biomet for the Warsaw medtech community.
items.imageAlt: Plug and Play graphic representing a Warsaw medtech networking event.

items.title: 2026 BioCrossroads Life Sciences Summit
items.summary: The 2026 BioCrossroads Life Sciences Summit will explore how artificial intelligence is changing research, development, manufacturing, commercialization, clinical care, and patient delivery.
items.imageAlt: BioCrossroads event graphic representing the 2026 Life Sciences Summit.

items.title: 2026 IBRI Global Forum
items.summary: The IBRI Global Forum will convene leaders in science, philanthropy, and investment to discuss translational medicine and drug development from discovery to patients.
items.imageAlt: William G. Kaelin Jr., keynote speaker for the 2026 IBRI Global Forum.
